title: Stop committing JSON-Schema-generated TS types; generate at build time
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Source: #1154 review item R28 (datadir/review-1154.md:227-236).

## Problem
The JSON-Schema-generated TypeScript type(s) for the stealth settings (produced by
`json-schema-to-typescript` / `json2ts` from the stealth settings schema) are committed
to the repo. The schema is the single source of truth; the type is derived and should be
generated at build time, not committed.

## Goal
- Add `json-schema-to-typescript` as a real `devDependency` (so `npm ci` installs it).
- Add a `prebuild`/`prepare` npm script that regenerates the type BEFORE anything that
  consumes it: `tsc`, webpack, lint, web-ext.
- Ensure CI runs codegen first.
- Verify NO flow (tsc / lint / web-ext) needs the file present without codegen having run.
- `.gitignore` the generated file and remove the committed copy.

## IMPORTANT context (why this was deferred OUT of #1154)
A naive wiring of the codegen step exposed a `build:main` (plain-`tsc`) failure:
`exportFunction` not found. Root cause: the plain-tsc build steps lack the Firefox
WebExtension type definitions — only the webpack/ts-loader build is exercised with those
types. So this is NOT just a prebuild script: the tsconfig / build-graph needs to be
untangled so every consuming build has both the generated type and the Firefox WebExtension
types available. Budget for build-graph work, not a one-line npm script.

## Scope / files
- The schema commit that introduced the committed type
- `Extension/package.json` (devDep + scripts)
- `.gitignore`
- tsconfig / `webpack.config.js` build graph
